target: Context Coherence as Retrieval Signal (Orthogonal V2)
methodology: edge-walk + fractal analysis on proposed Layer 5.45 context coherence injection

tier_1_full: Coherence-MMR interaction (complex state: two O(k^2) pairwise passes with opposing objectives)
Echo chamber formation (data integrity: coherence maximization can destroy result diversity)
Multi-hop query regression (data integrity: cross-session synthesis deliberately needs DIScoherent sets)
tier_2_standard: Pipeline ordering (high coupling: coherence interacts with MMR, cross-encoder, confidence pruning)
Entity overlap signal quality (external data: depends on entity extraction completeness)
Session map computation cost (performance: wavelet detection is O(n log n) on full store)
Coherence metric design (novel tech: no established pairwise set-coherence formula for memory retrieval)
tier_3_light: Temporal proximity signal (low coupling: simple timestamp subtraction, no state)
text_similarity reuse (low risk: already battle-tested in Layer 4.92)
Tracing/logging of coherence adjustments (observability: additive to existing Layer debug tracing)

boundary_details.name: Layer 5.0 scored list -> Coherence input (data handoff)
boundary_details.data_crossing: Vec<SharedMemory> with scores, entity_refs, experience.entities, created_at, embeddings. Session map via get_or_compute_session_map().
boundary_details.control_flow: Sequential: Layer 5.3 (cross-encoder) -> 5.35 (fragment demotion) -> 5.4 (MMR) -> PROPOSED 5.45 (coherence). Coherence sees POST-MMR results.
boundary_details.failure_mode: Session map computation fails -> coherence degrades to entity_overlap + temporal_proximity only (no same-session signal). Silent degradation.
boundary_details.contract: Input: sorted Vec<SharedMemory> (max_results items post-MMR). Output: same Vec, potentially reordered.
boundary_details.attack_surface: None new. Reads existing memory fields. No external I/O.

hazop.NO: Coherence disabled (e.g., single result) -> passthrough. Impact: zero. This is correct behavior.
hazop.MORE: All top-5 memories have high pairwise coherence -> no reordering needed. Coherence is a no-op for already-coherent sets. Good.
hazop.LESS: All top-5 memories have zero entity overlap, different sessions, distant timestamps -> coherence cannot help. No harm (passthrough), but no benefit.
hazop.REVERSE: CRITICAL: Coherence promotes memory B over memory C, but C was more individually relevant. Rank displacement without relevance justification.
hazop.PART_OF: entity_refs empty on many memories (extraction incomplete) -> coherence signal is partial. Only text_similarity and temporal signals remain.
hazop.OTHER_THAN: Coherence promotes a cluster of same-session memories that are topically related but do not answer the query. Echo chamber.
hazop.EARLY: Coherence applied BEFORE MMR -> MMR destroys coherence-driven ordering. Wasted computation.
hazop.LATE: Coherence applied AFTER ordinal resolution (5.9) -> could undo pinned ordinal positions. Destroys 5.9 guarantees.

fmea.failure: Coherence reordering undoes cross-encoder precision gains from Layer 5.3
fmea.notes: Cross-encoder spent 12% blend to get precise ordering. Coherence re-sort at 5.45 could move memories that cross-encoder promoted. Detectable via A/B benchmark comparison.

boundary_details.name: Coherence <-> MMR interaction (opposing objectives)
boundary_details.data_crossing: MMR uses embedding cosine similarity to PENALIZE similar candidates (diversity). Coherence uses entity overlap + session + temporal proximity to PROMOTE similar candidates (coherence). Same signal space, opposite objectives.
boundary_details.control_flow: If coherence at 5.45 (post-MMR): MMR diversifies first, then coherence re-clusters. Net effect: partially undoes MMR. If coherence at 5.35 (pre-MMR): coherence clusters first, then MMR breaks clusters. Net effect: partially undoes coherence.
boundary_details.failure_mode: Oscillating reorder: MMR pushes apart, coherence pulls together. Final ordering is an unstable compromise that satisfies neither objective optimally.
boundary_details.contract: MMR: lambda * relevance - (1-lambda) * max_similarity. Coherence: relevance + alpha * avg_coherence_with_selected. These are mathematically opposing on the similarity axis.
boundary_details.fractal_check: BREAK: This is the first time the pipeline has two consecutive O(k^2) pairwise passes with opposing optimization targets. All prior layers are either additive boosts (commutative) or hard filters (order-independent). Coherence + MMR is order-dependent and non-commutative.

hazop.NO: MMR skipped for Attribute queries -> coherence runs unopposed. May over-cluster attribute results.
hazop.MORE: MMR lambda=0.6 (strong diversity) + strong coherence weight -> severe oscillation. Each pass undoes ~40% of the other's work.
hazop.LESS: MMR lambda=0.7 (weak diversity) + weak coherence weight -> both signals are noise. Neither has enough strength to meaningfully reorder.
hazop.REVERSE: Coherence applied before MMR: coherence clusters session-4 memories together, MMR breaks them apart. The user wanted session-4 clustering.

fmea.failure: MMR and coherence fight to a draw, producing ordering worse than either alone
fmea.notes: HIGHEST RPN. This is the fundamental tension. No amount of tuning resolves it because the objectives are mathematically opposed on the similarity axis. The only resolution is to UNIFY them into a single pass.

boundary_details.name: Coherence signal: entity overlap (entity_refs + experience.entities)
boundary_details.data_crossing: entity_refs: Vec<EntityRef> (entity_id: Uuid, name: String, relation: String). experience.entities: Vec<String> (free-text tags). Jaccard similarity on tag sets.
boundary_details.contract: calculate_entity_overlap() returns 0.0..1.0. Two memories sharing all entities = 1.0. No overlap = 0.0.
boundary_details.failure_mode: Entity extraction is incomplete: many memories have empty entity_refs (populated only during record() via entity extraction). experience.entities is more reliable but coarser-grained.

hazop.PART_OF: CRITICAL: entity_refs are only populated when graph memory is active. Without GraphMemory, entity_refs = empty for ALL memories. Coherence signal degrades to experience.entities only (free-text tags).
hazop.OTHER_THAN: Two memories share entity 'PostgreSQL' but one discusses schema design and the other discusses backup procedures. Entity overlap = 1.0 but topical coherence is low. False positive.

fmea.failure: Entity overlap is a noisy proxy for topical coherence
fmea.notes: Entity overlap captures 'about the same things' but not 'about the same ASPECT of the same things'. Two memories about PostgreSQL (schema vs backup) have entity_overlap=1.0 but are topically incoherent.

boundary_details.name: Coherence signal: same-session (wavelet SessionMap)
boundary_details.data_crossing: SessionMap.memory_to_session: HashMap<MemoryId, usize> (1-based ordinal). Two memories are same-session if their ordinals match.
boundary_details.contract: Binary signal: same_session = memory_to_session[a] == memory_to_session[b]. SessionMap computed via detect_sessions() with 7200s gap threshold or adaptive variant.
boundary_details.failure_mode: Session map not yet computed -> get_or_compute_session_map() does full-store scan. O(n log n) but could be 50-200ms for large stores. Already cached via session_map_cache Mutex.

hazop.NO: Session map computation fails (all memories in one session, or store scan error) -> binary signal is always true or always false. Impact: signal becomes constant, no discriminative power.
hazop.MORE: Fine-grained sessions (many short sessions) -> same-session is rare, signal rarely fires. Coherence loses its strongest signal.
hazop.LESS: Coarse sessions (everything in one 8-hour session) -> same-session is always true. No discriminative power.
hazop.OTHER_THAN: Two memories in same 2-hour window but completely unrelated (e.g., bug report and lunch break note). Same-session but topically incoherent.

boundary_details.name: Coherence signal: temporal proximity (created_at delta)
boundary_details.data_crossing: chrono::DateTime<Utc> from each memory. Delta computed as (a.created_at - b.created_at).num_hours().abs().
boundary_details.contract: Continuous signal: temporal_proximity = 1.0 - (delta_hours / max_delta_hours).min(1.0). Memories created seconds apart = 1.0. Memories weeks apart = 0.0.

hazop.MORE: All candidate memories from same day -> temporal_proximity ~1.0 for all pairs. No discriminative power.
hazop.OTHER_THAN: Memories close in time but from different contexts (morning architecture discussion, afternoon unrelated debugging). Temporal proximity is high but semantic coherence is low.

boundary_details.name: Coherence signal: text_similarity (word-overlap cosine)
boundary_details.data_crossing: Memory content strings -> text_similarity() from temporal_facts.rs. Returns 0.0..1.0 cosine similarity of lowercased word frequency vectors.
boundary_details.contract: Already used in Layer 4.92 for interference detection (threshold 0.7). Reusing same function for coherence.
boundary_details.failure_mode: Short memories (<10 words) produce noisy similarity scores. Already handles empty strings (returns 0.0).
boundary_details.fractal_check: CONSISTENT: text_similarity is used at Layer 4.92 (opposition detection, threshold 0.7), temporal_facts (contradiction, threshold 0.85), and proposed coherence (no threshold, continuous). Pattern holds: same function, different thresholds per context.

hazop.REVERSE: High text_similarity + no semantic opposition = agreement/coherence. But high text_similarity could also indicate NEAR-DUPLICATE content that Layer 4.95 missed (prefix different but body similar). Coherence would promote duplicates.

boundary_details.name: Greedy selection algorithm (coherence-weighted MMR variant)
boundary_details.data_crossing: Iterative: selected set grows from 1 to k. Each step evaluates all remaining candidates against all selected. O(k^2 * signal_count) per query.
boundary_details.control_flow: For each candidate: compute avg_coherence = mean(coherence(candidate, selected_i) for all i in selected). MMR-coherence score = lambda * relevance + (1-lambda) * avg_coherence. Select argmax.
boundary_details.contract: Lambda controls relevance vs coherence trade-off. Lambda=1.0 = pure relevance (no coherence). Lambda=0.0 = pure coherence (ignore relevance).
boundary_details.failure_mode: Greedy selection is not globally optimal. A locally coherent pair at positions 1-2 may prevent a globally better set at positions 1-5. No backtracking.

hazop.MORE: k=10 with strong coherence -> O(100) pairwise comparisons per query. Each comparison needs entity_overlap + temporal + text_similarity. Budget: ~100 * 3 signals = 300 computations. At 1us each = 0.3ms. Acceptable.
hazop.REVERSE: First-selected memory has high relevance but low topical coherence with the rest of the candidate pool. All subsequent selections optimized for coherence WITH memory#1, pulling the entire set toward memory#1's topic. Anchor bias.

fmea.failure: Anchor bias: first memory determines coherence direction for entire result set
fmea.notes: HIGHEST RPN in the evaluation. The greedy algorithm selects memory#2 based on coherence with memory#1 only. Memory#3 based on avg coherence with {#1, #2}. Since #2 was chosen for coherence with #1, the set is biased toward #1's topic. For multi-hop queries ('Why did we change the database AND what was the impact?'), this pulls everything toward one hop.

boundary_details.name: Query-type gating (when to apply coherence)
boundary_details.data_crossing: QueryType enum from query_parser: Attribute, Temporal, Exploratory.
boundary_details.control_flow: Proposed: coherence enabled for Temporal and Exploratory, disabled for Attribute (same gating pattern as MMR at Layer 5.4).
boundary_details.failure_mode: Attribute queries skipped -> no regression risk for single-hop factual queries (currently MRR 1.000 across all 5 single_hop queries).

hazop.OTHER_THAN: Multi-hop queries classified as Exploratory -> coherence enabled. But multi-hop queries NEED diverse cross-session results. Coherence harms them. QueryType does not distinguish 'multi-hop' from 'exploratory'.
hazop.PART_OF: Temporal queries get coherence -> clustering same-session memories helps 'What happened during X?' but harms 'What was the most recent X?' (needs recency, not coherence).

fmea.failure: Multi-hop queries treated as Exploratory get coherence applied, destroying cross-session retrieval
fmea.notes: ALL-TIME HIGHEST RPN. The 5 multi-hop queries in LOCOMO have current MRR 0.900. Coherence would pull results toward one session/topic, likely dropping the second-hop memory. Expected MRR regression: 0.900 -> 0.700-0.800 for multi-hop. This is a BLOCKER.

boundary_details.name: Interaction with Layer 4.92 (interference detection)
boundary_details.data_crossing: Layer 4.92 detects CONTRADICTIONS via text_similarity > 0.7 + semantic_opposition. Coherence promotes HIGH text_similarity (agreement). These are complementary: 4.92 removes contradictions, coherence promotes agreements.
boundary_details.contract: 4.92 demotes penalty = sim * 0.15 for contradictions. Coherence promotes boost proportional to coherence score. Net effect: contradictory pairs get demoted by 4.92 AND receive low coherence score. Double penalty. Non-contradictory similar pairs get promoted by coherence.
boundary_details.fractal_check: CONSISTENT: 4.92 and coherence use the same signal (text_similarity) for opposite sides of the same coin. 4.92 handles the negative case (opposition), coherence handles the positive case (agreement). Clean separation.

hazop.NO: 4.92 misses a contradiction (text_similarity < 0.7 but content still contradicts) -> coherence may promote the contradictory pair if they share entities/session. Slight risk.
hazop.MORE: 4.92 and coherence both fire on the same pair -> double adjustment. Not harmful (demotion + low coherence score both push pair apart), but redundant computation.

boundary_details.name: Interaction with Layer 5.7 (confidence gating / score-gap pruning)
boundary_details.data_crossing: 5.7 prunes results where score < 25% of top_score OR step_ratio < 0.40. If coherence reordering changes scores, it changes the score distribution that 5.7 uses for pruning.
boundary_details.control_flow: If coherence at 5.45 and 5.7 at 5.7: coherence reorders, THEN 5.7 prunes based on new order. If coherence only reorders (no score change), 5.7 is unaffected.

hazop.REVERSE: Coherence promotes a low-scoring memory to position 2 (high coherence with #1 but low individual relevance). Score gap from #1 to #2 is now large. 5.7 may prune everything after #1. INFORMATION LOSS.

fmea.failure: Coherence reordering creates score gaps that trigger aggressive pruning at 5.7
fmea.notes: Only if coherence modifies scores rather than just reordering. If coherence is order-only (no score change), 5.7 operates on original scores and this interaction is neutral.

boundary_details.name: Interaction with Layer 5.9 (ordinal resolution)
boundary_details.data_crossing: 5.9 pins memories at specific ranks (e.g., earliest at rank 1 for 'first X' queries). If coherence runs BEFORE 5.9, its ordering is preserved by 5.9's pin. If AFTER, coherence could unpin.
boundary_details.contract: 5.85 linguistic boost was explicitly moved BEFORE 5.9 'to avoid undoing pins'. Coherence must follow the same pattern.
boundary_details.fractal_check: CONSISTENT: The pattern is 'all reordering before 5.9, all pins at 5.9'. Coherence at 5.45 follows this pattern.

hazop.LATE: Coherence applied after 5.9 -> unpins ordinal resolution. 'What was the first bug?' loses its pin. REGRESSION.
hazop.NO: Coherence disabled for ordinal queries -> safe. But ordinal detection happens at 5.9, not at coherence layer. Would need to forward-detect ordinals.

phase_2_fractal_atam.core_pattern: Pairwise candidate comparison with greedy selection — structural analog of apply_mmr() but with coherence instead of diversity as the inter-candidate signal

macro_consistency.detail: The pipeline has a clear directional flow: signals narrow the candidate set progressively. Layer 2 (graph) expands, Layer 3 (vector) expands, Layer 4 (fusion) merges, Layers 5+ score/filter/reorder. Coherence at 5.45 is the ONLY layer that re-expands the selection criterion (preferring candidates based on INTER-candidate relationships rather than QUERY-candidate relationships). This breaks the convergent flow pattern.
macro_consistency.impact: Every other post-fusion layer asks 'how well does this memory match the query?' Coherence asks 'how well does this memory match other selected memories?' Different question, different optimization target. Structural foreign body.

meso_consistency.detail: The O(k^2) greedy selection pattern matches apply_mmr() exactly. Both iterate over remaining candidates, compute pairwise scores against the selected set, and pick argmax. The difference is the sign: MMR subtracts similarity (diversity), coherence adds similarity (clustering). Same algorithm, opposite polarity. This is clean from an implementation perspective but semantically contradictory.
matching_patterns: Greedy selection: apply_mmr() and proposed apply_coherence() have identical loop structure
Pairwise scoring: both compute max/avg similarity to selected set
Query-type gating: both use QueryType to decide whether to fire

micro_consistency.detail: Coherence signals (entity_overlap, temporal_proximity, text_similarity, same_session) reuse existing infrastructure without modification. No new data structures, no new storage, no new APIs. Pure computation over existing fields.

breaks.description: MMR at 5.4 optimizes for diversity (penalize similarity). Coherence at 5.45 optimizes for coherence (promote similarity). These are mathematically opposed on the embedding similarity axis. The pipeline cannot simultaneously maximize both. The ordering of 5.4 then 5.45 means coherence partially undoes MMR, producing an unstable compromise.
resolution_options: OPTION A: Unify into single pass. Modify apply_mmr() to use a 3-way trade-off: lambda_relevance * relevance - lambda_diversity * max_similarity + lambda_coherence * avg_coherence. Single pass, no oscillation.
OPTION B: Remove MMR when coherence is active. Replace MMR with coherence-aware selection. But this loses diversity protection entirely.
OPTION C: Sequential with dampening. Coherence at 5.45 can only promote memories by at most 1 position (bounded displacement). Limits damage from oscillation but also limits benefit.

breaks.description: Multi-hop queries require deliberately DIScoherent result sets (memory about database change + memory about timeline impact are from different sessions, different entities, different temporal periods). Coherence would penalize exactly the diversity that multi-hop queries need. The QueryType enum has no 'MultiHop' variant — multi-hop is classified as Exploratory and gets full coherence treatment.
resolution_options: OPTION A: Add QueryType::MultiHop detection (queries with 'AND', 'impact', 'led to', 'because of'). Disable coherence for multi-hop.
OPTION B: Gate coherence on query DECOMPOSITION. If query decomposes into 2+ sub-queries, disable coherence (sub-queries need diverse results).
OPTION C: Use coherence only as a TIE-BREAKER, not a primary reranking signal. Only promote when two candidates have identical (within epsilon) relevance scores.

breaks.description: Greedy coherence selection anchors on the first-selected memory (highest relevance). All subsequent selections are biased toward coherence with #1. For queries where the best answer spans multiple topics ('Summarize the project'), this collapses the result set to #1's topic neighborhood.
resolution_options: OPTION A: Bidirectional coherence. Instead of avg_coherence(candidate, selected), use min_coherence(candidate, selected). This prevents anchor domination — candidate must be coherent with ALL selected, not just #1.
OPTION B: Coherence windowing. Only consider coherence with the last 2 selected memories, not the full selected set. Limits anchor reach.
OPTION C: Pre-cluster then select. Cluster candidates by coherence FIRST, then select top-1 from each cluster. Prevents greedy anchor bias.

tradeoffs: Coherence (clustering) vs Diversity (spreading) — fundamentally opposed objectives that cannot be simultaneously maximized. Must choose one as primary.
Set quality (coherent narrative) vs Individual quality (each memory maximally relevant) — coherence sacrifices individual relevance for set-level coherence.
Temporal clustering (same-session preference) vs Temporal coverage (span the full timeline) — coherence prefers temporal locality, harming summary queries.
Entity focus (same entities = coherent) vs Entity breadth (more entities = more informative) �� coherence narrows entity coverage.
Computational cost (O(k^2) additional pass) vs Latency budget (pipeline already at 60-110ms) — coherence adds ~0.5ms for k=10, acceptable.

challenges.target: Claim: coherence helps temporal queries like 'What decisions did we make last week?'
challenges.adversary_argument: The current failure (MRR 0.500, memory 58 at rank 1 instead of 46/49/52) is NOT a coherence problem. Memory 58 (SDK decision) is from session 4, same as 46/49/52. All four are same-session decisions. Coherence would promote 58 even more because it shares session and 'decision' entity overlap with 46/49/52. The actual problem is that 58's RECENCY score or BM25 score is higher, not that it's incoherent with the expected set.
challenges.evidence: Memory 58 (session 4), Memory 52 (session 4), Memory 46 (session 4), Memory 49 (session 4) — ALL in the same session. Same-session coherence signal is 1.0 for all pairs. Entity overlap between 58 and {46,49,52} is non-zero ('decision' tag shared). Coherence cannot distinguish 58 from 46/49/52 because they ARE coherent.
challenges.verdict: CONFIRMED. Coherence does NOT help this query. The problem is relevance ranking, not set coherence. Expected MRR change: 0.000.

challenges.target: Claim: coherence helps 'What bugs did we find during the debugging phase?' (R5 0.375)
challenges.adversary_argument: Expected memories are [30,31,32,33,34,36,37,41] — 8 debugging-phase memories. Retrieved: [41,48,35,30,36,32,40,34,31,42]. Memory 48 (microservice bugs, session 4) infiltrates at rank 2. Coherence COULD help here: 48 is from session 4 while the debugging phase (session 3) memories would form a coherent cluster. Same-session signal would prefer session-3 bug memories over session-4 bug memories.
challenges.evidence: Retrieved memory 48 is session 4. Expected memories are session 3 (30-37) and session 4 (41). Memory 41 (session 3, id 41) is correctly at rank 1. Coherence with session-3 cluster would demote 48 from rank 2. BUT: memory 35 (session 3) and memory 40 (session 3) are already in top-10 but not in expected set. Coherence would promote these noise session-3 memories too.
challenges.verdict: PARTIAL. Coherence helps by demoting session-4 intruder (48) but also promotes session-3 noise (35, 40 which are not in expected set). Net R5 improvement estimated: +0.05 to +0.10. Modest.

challenges.target: Claim: coherence won't cause echo chambers
challenges.adversary_argument: For 'What patterns do you see in our decision-making?' (expected [52, 59]), the retrieved set is [46, 52, 49, 58, 3, 18, 16, 43, 2, 1]. Memory 59 (lessons learned) is absent from top-10. Coherence between 46 (MongoDB reversal, session 4) and the other session-4 decisions is HIGH. Coherence would promote MORE session-4 decisions instead of pulling in memory 59 (also session 4, also shares 'decision' entities). Actually, 59 IS session 4. So coherence might pull it in IF it has entity overlap with the selected set. But 59's tags are ['lessons', 'retrospective', 'growth'] — no overlap with 46's tags ['architecture-change', 'mongodb', 'postgresql', 'reversed']. Entity overlap is 0.0. Coherence would NOT promote 59.
challenges.evidence: Memory 59 tags: ['lessons', 'retrospective', 'growth']. Memory 46 tags: ['architecture-change', 'mongodb', 'postgresql', 'reversed']. Jaccard overlap: 0/7 = 0.0. Same session (4), but entity overlap is zero. text_similarity would be moderate (shared project vocabulary) but not high enough to overcome the relevance gap.
challenges.verdict: CONFIRMED echo chamber risk. Coherence promotes more session-4 decisions (49, 58, 52) while the desired memory 59 has zero entity overlap with the decision cluster. The result set becomes a homogeneous 'session 4 decisions' cluster that excludes the meta-reflective 'lessons learned' memory.

challenges.target: Claim: Layer 4.92 interference detection is insufficient and coherence fills the gap
challenges.adversary_argument: 4.92 handles CONTRADICTIONS (text_similarity > 0.7 AND semantic_opposition). Coherence claims to handle AGREEMENT (promote coherent pairs). But the gap between 'not contradictory' and 'coherent' is not well-defined. Two memories that don't contradict each other are not necessarily coherent — they might be on completely different topics. The actual gap is: 4.92 handles one failure mode (contradictions in top-5), but the claimed failure mode (disjointed top-5) has no evidence in the benchmark data.
challenges.evidence: Examining all 20 LOCOMO queries: ZERO cases where a contradictory pair appears in top-5 (4.92 is working). The 'disjointed set' claim assumes that topical coherence improves downstream answer quality, but the benchmark measures R@5 and MRR — both are INDIVIDUAL memory metrics, not set-level metrics. A disjointed but individually relevant top-5 scores identically to a coherent top-5 on MRR/R@5.
challenges.verdict: CONFIRMED. The benchmark metrics (MRR, R@5, R@10, P@5) do not measure set coherence. A coherence signal optimizes for a property that the benchmark cannot detect. The claimed benefit is unmeasurable with current evaluation infrastructure.

phase_3_scoring.proposal_evaluated: Layer 5.45: Context Coherence Reranking (greedy coherence-weighted selection post-MMR)

correctness.evidence_code: The greedy selection algorithm is mathematically sound (same structure as apply_mmr). Entity overlap via Jaccard is a valid similarity metric. text_similarity is proven in Layer 4.92. Session detection is stable via wavelet analysis.
correctness.evidence_structural: BUT the optimization target (set coherence) is not aligned with retrieval objectives (individual relevance). The algorithm is correct for what it computes, but what it computes is not what the system needs. MRR/R@5 measure individual memory placement, not set coherence.

robustness.evidence_code: Graceful degradation when session_map fails (falls back to entity + temporal). Empty entity_refs handled (returns 0.0 overlap). No panics in the critical path.
robustness.evidence_structural: BLOCKER RISK: Multi-hop query regression. 5 multi-hop queries at current MRR 0.900 would likely regress to 0.700-0.800 because coherence pulls results toward one hop's topic. No QueryType::MultiHop to gate this. Also: anchor bias in greedy selection has no mitigation — first memory determines coherence direction for entire set.

security.evidence_code: No new I/O, no new state, no new API surface. Pure computation over existing memory fields. No injection vectors.
security.evidence_structural: Reads entity_refs, experience.entities, created_at, embeddings — all internal, immutable during retrieval.

performance.evidence_code: O(k^2) where k = max_results (typically 5-10). For k=10: 45 pairwise comparisons * 4 signals each = 180 operations. At ~1us per operation: 0.18ms. Current pipeline is 60-110ms. Additional latency: 0.2-0.5ms. Negligible.
performance.evidence_structural: Session map already cached via session_map_cache Mutex. Entity overlap is Jaccard on small sets (<20 tags). text_similarity is word-frequency cosine (fast). No new I/O, no new allocations beyond stack temporaries.

maintainability.evidence_code: Implementation mirrors apply_mmr() structure — same greedy loop, same normalization, same query-type gating. Developers familiar with MMR can read coherence code immediately.
maintainability.evidence_structural: BUT: adds another O(k^2) pass to an already deep pipeline (12+ layers). Interaction with MMR is subtle and non-obvious. Future developers must understand that 5.4 (MMR) and 5.45 (coherence) have opposing objectives.

data_integrity.evidence_code: No mutation of stored data. All operations are read-only score recomputation. Original scores preserved (coherence only reorders, does not modify scores if implemented as order-only).
data_integrity.evidence_structural: RISK: Coherence can demote individually relevant memories in favor of collectively coherent but less relevant ones. For queries with a single critical memory (e.g., 'What database are we using?' -> memory 3), coherence could push the critical memory down if it's not coherent with the rest of top-5. However, Attribute queries are gated off, so this specific failure is prevented.

scalability.evidence_code: O(k^2) where k is bounded by max_results (configurable, typically 5-10). Does not scale with corpus size. Session map is O(n log n) but cached.
scalability.evidence_structural: For k=20 (rerank pool): 190 pairwise comparisons. Still under 1ms. For k=50: 1225 comparisons, ~1-2ms. Acceptable scaling.

testability.evidence_code: Can unit test the coherence scoring function in isolation. Can test pairwise signal computation (entity_overlap, temporal_proximity, same_session, text_similarity) individually.
testability.evidence_structural: BUT: the key question (does set coherence improve retrieval quality?) requires benchmark-level evaluation with manually labeled 'coherent set' ground truth. Current LOCOMO/Nexus benchmarks measure individual memory retrieval, not set coherence. No evaluation infrastructure exists to measure the claimed benefit.

extensibility.evidence_code: Coherence signals are pluggable: entity_overlap, temporal_proximity, same_session, text_similarity can be weighted independently. New signals (e.g., same_episode, causal_chain) can be added without structural changes.
extensibility.evidence_structural: The greedy selection framework is general-purpose. Could be parameterized with different coherence functions for different query types.

compliance.evidence_code: No new data collection, no new PII handling, no new external communication. Pure internal computation.
compliance.evidence_structural: Uses existing memory fields only. No regulatory implications.

coherence.evidence_code: IRONIC: a feature called 'context coherence' has low coherence with the existing pipeline. MMR at 5.4 actively fights coherence at 5.45. The pipeline's macro pattern (convergent query-candidate scoring) is broken by inter-candidate scoring. The optimization target (set coherence) is unmeasurable by existing benchmarks (MRR, R@5).
coherence.evidence_structural: The fundamental issue: the retrieval pipeline is designed to answer 'which individual memories best match this query?' Coherence asks 'which SET of memories is most internally consistent?' These are different questions. Adding set-level optimization to an individual-level pipeline creates an architectural chimera.

usability.evidence_code: If implemented, coherence is invisible to the user (no new API parameters, no new configuration). Auto-applied based on query type.
usability.evidence_structural: But the user cannot observe when coherence helps or harms. No logging of 'coherence promoted memory X over memory Y'. No feedback mechanism to tune coherence vs relevance trade-off per query.

blockers: Robustness (4.5): Multi-hop query regression with no QueryType::MultiHop gate. Expected MRR regression 0.900 -> 0.700-0.800 on 5 multi-hop queries.
Coherence-with-pipeline (3.5): Opposing objectives with MMR, unmeasurable benefit with existing benchmarks, breaks macro convergent-flow pattern.

quality.notes: Algorithm is sound but optimizes for the wrong target. Set coherence is not measured by MRR/R@5/P@5. Cannot demonstrate improvement on existing benchmarks.

completeness.notes: Missing: multi-hop query gating, benchmark metrics for set coherence, anchor bias mitigation, MMR unification strategy. The proposal addresses one half of the problem (promoting coherence) without addressing the other half (when NOT to promote coherence).

performance.notes: 0.2-0.5ms additional latency for O(k^2) pairwise comparison. Well within budget. Reuses cached session map and existing similarity functions.

utility.notes: UNMEASURABLE utility. Current benchmarks cannot detect set-coherence improvements. Would need a new evaluation metric (e.g., 'narrative quality score' or 'answer completeness from top-5'). Without measurable utility, the feature cannot be validated or tuned.

coherence.notes: Structural foreign body in a convergent pipeline. Opposing objectives with MMR. Breaks the 'every layer scores query-vs-candidate' pattern by introducing candidate-vs-candidate scoring.

cohesiveness.notes: Implementation structure mirrors apply_mmr() (good). But architectural role contradicts apply_mmr() (bad). The code fits but the semantics clash.

elasticity.notes: Coherence weight is tunable. Query-type gating is configurable. Individual signals can be weighted. But no runtime API to adjust coherence parameters.

function.notes: Achieves set coherence (as defined) but at the cost of multi-hop regression, anchor bias, and echo chamber formation. The function works but the side effects outweigh the primary effect.

ease_of_use.notes: Zero-config auto-application with query-type gating. But no observability into coherence decisions, no user feedback mechanism.

critical.title: DO NOT implement context coherence as a separate pipeline layer
critical.description: The fundamental flaw is that coherence (promote similarity) and MMR (penalize similarity) are mathematically opposed when implemented as sequential pipeline stages. Adding a second O(k^2) pass with opposite polarity creates an unstable compromise worse than either signal alone. This is not fixable by tuning — it is a structural conflict.
critical.impact: Prevents multi-hop MRR regression (estimated 0.900 -> 0.700), prevents echo chamber formation, avoids adding complexity for unmeasurable benefit.
critical.recommendation: REJECT the proposal in its current form.

critical.title: Instead: unify coherence INTO existing MMR as a 3-way trade-off
critical.description: Modify apply_mmr() to compute: score = lambda_r * relevance - lambda_d * max_similarity + lambda_c * avg_coherence(entity_overlap + same_session). Single O(k^2) pass. lambda_c gated to 0.0 for multi-hop (detected via query decomposition or AND/because keywords). For temporal queries, lambda_c = 0.1 (mild coherence preference). For exploratory, lambda_c = 0.05.
critical.impact: Gets the benefit of coherence (mild same-session preference) without the structural conflict. Single pass eliminates oscillation. Query-type gating prevents multi-hop regression.

implementation_sketch.change: Add coherence_weight: f32 parameter. Inside the greedy loop, after computing max_sim, also compute avg_coherence with selected set using entity_overlap + same_session. New formula: lambda * relevance - (1-lambda-cw) * max_sim + cw * avg_coherence. When cw=0 (multi-hop), behavior is identical to current MMR.

high.title: Add QueryType::MultiHop detection
high.description: Detect multi-hop queries via: (1) presence of AND/OR conjunctions between distinct clauses, (2) causal language ('because', 'led to', 'impact of', 'as a result'), (3) query decomposition producing 2+ sub-queries. Tag as MultiHop to gate coherence and adjust MMR lambda.
high.impact: Protects multi-hop MRR 0.900 from regression. Required for any form of coherence integration.

high.title: Build set-coherence benchmark metric
high.description: Cannot validate coherence without measuring it. Add metrics: (1) narrative_coverage: does the top-5 set contain all expected answer components? (2) set_redundancy: what fraction of top-5 is near-duplicate or same-fact? (3) answer_completeness: LLM-judged score of 'can the top-5 fully answer the query?'
high.impact: Makes coherence measurable. Without this, any coherence feature is untestable speculation.

medium.title: Strengthen Layer 4.92 as the 'negative coherence' signal
medium.description: Layer 4.92 already detects contradictions but the penalty is mild (sim * 0.15 max). For high-confidence contradictions (sim > 0.9 AND semantic_opposition), increase penalty to 0.30. This is the safe, measurable form of coherence: removing incoherent (contradictory) pairs rather than promoting coherent pairs.
medium.impact: Directly addresses the stated problem ('two memories that contradict each other should not both be in top-5') without the diversity trade-off. Much safer than positive coherence promotion.

medium.title: Session-aware tie-breaking (minimal coherence signal)
medium.description: When two candidate memories have scores within epsilon (0.005 of each other), prefer the one that shares a session with the highest-ranked memory. This is coherence as a TIE-BREAKER only — it never overrides relevance, only disambiguates ties. Zero risk of multi-hop regression.
medium.impact: Captures the essence of coherence (prefer same-session when relevance is equal) without any of the structural risks. Expected benefit: marginal but safe.

low.title: Entity cluster pre-computation for future coherence work
low.description: During record(), compute and cache entity clusters (groups of memories sharing 2+ entities). Store as entity_cluster_id on each memory. Future coherence signals can use cluster membership as a O(1) signal instead of O(k^2) pairwise comparison.
low.impact: Infrastructure for future work. No immediate benefit.

verdict.recommendation: REJECT the proposal as specified. ADOPT the alternative approach (COHERE-02 + COHERE-06).
verdict.rationale: Context coherence as a separate post-MMR pipeline layer is structurally unsound. It creates opposing objectives with the existing MMR diversification pass (RPN 224), causes multi-hop regression (RPN 336), introduces anchor bias (RPN 280), and optimizes for a property unmeasurable by existing benchmarks. The three highest-RPN risks in this evaluation are ALL from the coherence-as-separate-layer design.
what_to_do_instead: 1. Strengthen Layer 4.92 (COHERE-05): increase contradiction penalty for high-confidence oppositions. This directly solves 'contradictions in top-5' with zero risk. Trivial effort.
2. Add session-aware tie-breaking (COHERE-06): when two candidates have near-identical scores, prefer same-session. Zero regression risk. Trivial effort.
3. Unify into MMR (COHERE-02): add coherence_weight parameter to apply_mmr() for a single-pass 3-way trade-off. Low effort, requires QueryType::MultiHop detection (COHERE-03).
4. Build set-coherence metrics (COHERE-04): before any coherence feature, build the evaluation infrastructure to measure whether it helps. High effort but necessary for validated work.

expected_impact_of_alternatives.multi_hop_safety: No regression (all changes are gated or tie-breaker-only)
expected_impact_of_alternatives.complexity: Net reduction — COHERE-05 is a constant change, COHERE-06 is 10 lines of code, COHERE-02 adds ~20 lines to existing function

verdict.key_insight: The problem statement ('promote coherent sets') is correct but the solution ('add another pipeline layer') is wrong. Coherence is not a LAYER — it is a DIMENSION of the existing MMR diversification. The right place for coherence is INSIDE apply_mmr(), not after it. Diversity and coherence are two ends of the same spectrum, and they belong in the same formula, not in sequential competing passes.
